location.office_time_required: 3 days per week on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays

secondary_archetype: Technical PM (marketplace/algorithmic pricing, people manager GPM)

seniority.level: Staff / Principal IC or Senior Manager (GPM II, managing a team of PMs)
level_signals: 8+ years of product management or relevant experience
2+ years of experience managing product managers
Manages a team of PMs across four sub-teams
Comp range $208K-$260K base consistent with senior people manager PM at Lyft
Interfaces directly with Lyft executive team

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years of product management or relevant experience, with significant time spent on algorithmic, data-driven, or marketplace products
must_haves.qualifier: with significant time on algorithmic, data-driven, or marketplace products

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 2+ years of experience managing product managers
A deep technical understanding of microeconomics, and ideally a background in Operations Research, Machine Learning, or algorithmic systems
Demonstrated experience managing and growing a high performing team of product managers while providing development, mentorship, and excitement to exceed team and department goals
Experience working in deep product domains — using data and experimentation to guide a large cross-functional team towards a long-term vision
Strong communication, presentation, and stakeholder management skills across a diverse group of stakeholders, from partner teams to executive levels
Ability to dive deep into complex technical concepts, but also abstract them to executives, stakeholders, operations, and legal
Maintain a strong POV on what to build for users, amidst many different stakeholders with different goals and world-class scientists + engineers on the team with their own opinions
Experience running A/B tests and using common statistical techniques to evaluate results, including developing post-launch testing strategies
Passion for Lyft and what we are trying to achieve in sustainable urban transportation

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: ideally a background in Operations Research, Machine Learning, or algorithmic systems

scope.team_size_estimate: team of PMs across four sub-teams (Planned Pricing, Dynamic Pricing & Offer Selection, Rider Engagement, Price Orchestration)
scope.surface: End-to-end pricing lifecycle: base fare strategy, real-time dynamic pricing, rider engagement/incentives, and pricing platform infrastructure
scope.business_model: marketplace / two-sided platform (rider demand, driver supply), B2C rideshare
core_responsibilities: Manage and grow a team of product managers, providing development, mentorship, and coaching
Drive strategic product and technology vision for Pricing, owning the system that directly impacts Gross Bookings, Active Riders, and Adjusted EBITDA Margin
Set and align on business goals for pricing optimization with executives and business teams
Incorporate end-to-end impact of pricing decisions on riders, drivers, and network growth
Define short-term and long-term strategy, set team success metrics and roadmap
Develop and execute product roadmap via collaboration with senior leadership and cross-functional partners
Review how PMs translate customer needs into functional and technical requirements
Guide data science, analysts, and engineers to refine scope, prioritize projects, and define opportunities
Serve as an inspiring communicator in team all-hands and executive reviews

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($208K-$260K base, SF area)
Clear team structure with four named sub-teams and ownership areas
Explicit metrics ownership (Gross Bookings, Active Riders, Adjusted EBITDA Margin)
High strategic visibility — direct interaction with Lyft's executive team
Detailed responsibilities that signal a well-scoped role, not exploratory headcount
Hybrid flexibility with up to 4 weeks work-from-anywhere per year
red_flags: No visa sponsorship information stated — unclear for international candidates
Requires in-office 3 days/week (Mon/Wed/Thu) in San Francisco — limited flexibility for non-Bay Area candidates

The 'ideally' qualifier on Operations Research/ML background is noted as a nice-to-have but is listed within the required Experience section — could be treated as harder than soft by some hiring managers
No explicit mention of compensation for locations outside San Francisco despite hybrid/flexible framing

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, 8+ years requirement, and GPM II title with multi-team people management scope)
